encounter.reason: brain MRI for Alzheimer's disease monitoring, comparison to prior study
encounter.location: Emory Cognitive Neurology Center, 12 Executive Park Drive NE, Suite 200, Atlanta, GA 30329

procedures.name: MRI of brain without and with intravenous gadolinium contrast
procedures.reason: Alzheimer's disease monitoring, progressive cognitive decline, comparison to April 2022 brain MRI

document_references.description: Formal radiology report for MRI brain without and with contrast performed 2025-04-02. Documents progressive bilateral hippocampal atrophy (now moderate), mildly progressive generalized cortical atrophy most prominent in temporal and parietal lobes, chronic small-vessel ischemic white matter changes (Fazekas grade 1–2), and absence of acute pathology. Compared to April 2022 study from Jacksonville.

diagnostic_reports.type: MRI of brain without and with contrast
diagnostic_reports.description: Imaging report with structured impression. Findings include: (1) progressive neurodegenerative changes consistent with Alzheimer's disease with moderate bilateral hippocampal atrophy progressed from mild on April 2022 comparison; (2) mildly progressive generalized cortical atrophy, most prominent in temporal and parietal lobes; (3) chronic small-vessel ischemic white matter changes, Fazekas grade 1–2, mildly progressed; (4) no acute infarct, intracranial hemorrhage, mass lesion, or hydrocephalus. Results forwarded to Dr. Nakamura for review. Clinical correlation with neurocognitive testing recommended.
